weather: 12–14°C, fog, mostly_clear, overcast, rain, wind 10–17 km/h
narrative: At dawn, I listened to the gentle rustling of leaves as California Towhees scratched through the grassy undergrowth in Tilden Regional Park. The birdsong was a melodic chorus that filled the air, interspersed with calls from American Crows perched on nearby utility poles. As the day progressed, the California Towhees' presence became more pronounced at dawn and evening hours, while American Crows were active throughout the morning.  First detection of American Crow in the monitoring window. Towhee breeding season just opened — dawn calls are territorial boundary checks. Junco April absence = normal spring departure to Sierra, not rain. Watch for Orange-crowned Warbler on clearing mornings.
